displayTitle: One Shot, Two Films, Any Shape

beats.narration: A thirty six inch girth weld in one exposure? Source in the middle, film wrapped outside — that's panoramic radiography.

payload.title: THE PANORAMIC SHOT
payload.sub: One exposure covers the whole girth weld.

beats.narration: The trick is dead center: every point sees the same distance, so density runs uniform. Drift off axis — near side dark, far side light.

payload.heading: Centered source, uniform density
payload.note: OFF CENTER → NEAR SIDE DARK · FAR SIDE LIGHT

beats.narration: Load two films — fast plus slow spans a thickness range, same speed makes duplicate records, and composite pairs build density on the viewer.

payload.heading: Three reasons to load two films

beats.narration: Parts are rarely flat plates. Keep film close, beam square, path length steady — and mask thin casting edges to absorb scatter.

payload.heading: The part's shape drives the shot

chips.t: Path length steady across the film
Mask thin edges to absorb scatter

beats.narration: Read the part first, the exposure chart second — and write the shot plan before anyone pushes a source out of the camera.

payload.sub: Write it down before the source leaves the camera.
